name: The White House
subject: Sanctuaries for Americans, not criminal aliens
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=adf0b9d0b682f81f63c261b08807449b96449967768911a9f6db4e029065d7508cc7e95771cb756e9dcd72fd4d4aaaef1b008ee6eba1978d

Sanctuaries for Americans, not criminal aliens

“We're going to take care of the problem," President Donald J. Trump told a room full of Cabinet officials, elected leaders, and law enforcement representatives during a roundtable discussion on sanctuary cities at the White House yesterday.

The President’s highest duty is to protect the safety and well-being of American citizens. Lawless sanctuary jurisdictions nullify Federal law, obstruct immigration enforcement, and release thousands of criminals into U.S. communities. “They are not sanctuary cities; they are outlaw cities,” Sen. Tom Cotton (R-AR) said at yesterday’s meeting.

The Senator is right, and the Trump Administration has taken action to ensure our Nation’s laws are faithfully enforced. During the 2017 fiscal year, ICE’s Enforcement and Removal Operations team made more than 140,000 administrative arrests and effected more than 225,000 removals.

Learn more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=adf0b9d0b682f81f76ca72ef2e91c41d3b101c395b1bf1577c65ed48b012e81ad8459509786ec20884b887e8f67741ba02cf89d6ec815c6a
“Our cities should be sanctuaries for Americans—not for criminal aliens,” President Trump says.

What they’re saying about President Trump’s opioids plan

Monday afternoon, President Trump and the First Lady visited Manchester, New Hampshire, to talk through the ways this Administration is confronting America’s devastating opioid epidemic. The President’s initiative has three pillars: 1) reduce demand and over-prescription, 2) cut off the supply of illicit drugs, and 3) help those already struggling with addiction.

In the days since, a number of officials have weighed in on the plan:

- Speaker of the House Paul Ryan (R-WI): “The opioid epidemic continues to ravage our communities, and President Trump is right to continue the fight against it.”

- House Majority Leader Kevin McCarthy (R-CA) tweeted “.@POTUS’ proposals to combat opioid addiction demonstrate that there is ample opportunity to reach a bipartisan consensus.”

- Gov. Jim Justice (R-WV): “People are dying every day from opioid abuse. I’m encouraged and hopeful that @POTUS’s plans will help us take bigger steps to resolve this scourge on our society.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=adf0b9d0b682f81fda20f49c150aeafc31ff9293a2d215fe2ccd4080f8a4c8e692e9f4465ccf6dfbecea94cfe2002704b477db91e2b228e4
See what other leaders are saying about President Trump’s war on opioid abuse.

Join us to fight against the stigma of addiction. Share your two-minute video about how your life has been impacted by the opioid epidemic.

name: Chris McDaniel
subject: Remember Mississippi

body:
If we are going to drain the swamp in Washington I need the support of conservatives like you. Being a proven conservative hasn’t won me many friends in Washington, which means the only way we can win this fight is through the power of grassroots support. Our campaign will not have the benefit of being bankrolled by special interest groups or the Washington establishment.

Peter,

If we are going to drain the swamp in Washington I need the support of conservatives like you.

Being a proven conservative hasn’t won me many friends in Washington, which means the only way we can win this fight is through the power of grassroots support. Our campaign will not have the benefit of being bankrolled by special interest groups or the Washington establishment.

That’s why I’m asking you to help me lead the fight to bring conservative values back to Washington, by chipping in to our Remember Mississippi Fund today. [https://mcdaniel2018.com/contribute/?src=hf_mcdaniel_180228_WD2&utm_source=hf&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=180228_WD2]

Chip in $50 [https://mcdaniel2018.com/contribute/?src=hf_mcdaniel_180228_WD2&utm_source=hf&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=180228_WD2]

Like you, I'm absolutely horrified at how the Republican Party has lost its way in recent years.

Far too often, the deeply cherished conservative values that form the bedrock of American culture--free markets and limited government--are being undermined...

...not just by the liberal left, but from the establishment in our own party.

But you and I both know: 2018 is the last, best chance we've got to secure the future of our nation.

It's a fight we must win, and it's a fight we will win.

So please join me in taking our country back in 2018, by chipping in to our Remember Mississippi Fund so I can take our shared values to the United States Senate. [https://mcdaniel2018.com/contribute/?src=hf_mcdaniel_180228_WD2&utm_source=hf&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=180228_WD2]

Chip in today! [https://mcdaniel2018.com/contribute/?src=hf_mcdaniel_180228_WD2&utm_source=hf&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=180228_WD2]

Thank you,

Chris McDaniel

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Every vote matters
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3159/9e00690/4548974e/24025fe2/2262144490/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180320_Selma_acq_gq_act__AG&source=em18_180320_Selma_acq_gq_act__AG")
Friend,

In the last few months, Democrats have seen historic wins, and one thing is more clear than ever: every vote matters.

Just last week, Conor Lamb won by less than 650 votes in a district that Trump carried by around 20 points in 2016. And like so many other races that have flipped seats from red to blue in places like Alabama, Kentucky, Virginia -- every single vote mattered too.

But just 53 years ago, not everyone had the right to vote.

In March of 1965, thousands of demonstrators faced violence as they marched from Selma, Alabama, to Montgomery, Alabama demanding the right to vote for black people. Thanks to their courageous actions, five months later President Lyndon Johnson signed the Voting Rights Act of 1965.

**[Voting is a right that not everyone has, so we must use it. Make the commitment to vote in this year's primary and general elections -- then share to ask your friends and family who are eligible to do the same.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3159/9e00690/4548974e/24025fe5/2262144490/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-commit-to-vote?utm_campaign=em18_180320_Selma_acq_gq_act__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em18_180320_Selma_acq_gq_act__AG&firstname=&lastname=&zip=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_content=2+-+Voting+is+a+right+that+not+everyone+has+")**

From Martin Luther King's March on Washington in 1963 to the Parkland students' gun reform activism, Americans have used marches to protest inaction and discrimination by our government for years.

We have faced discrimination, voter suppression, and outright physical barriers to prevent us from getting to the voting booth. But when we mobilize, we can truly change the outcomes. We can change our government and future for the better.

**[So add your name and commit to voting this August and November -- then share with your friends and family to make sure they make their voices are heard too.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3159/9e00690/4548974e/24025fe4/2262144490/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-commit-to-vote?utm_campaign=em18_180320_Selma_acq_gq_act__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em18_180320_Selma_acq_gq_act__AG&firstname=&lastname=&zip=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_content=3+-+So+add+your+name+and+commit+to+voting+th")**

Thank you,

Andrew

| | **_IndyStar_ :
"Indiana race for U.S. Senate generates most advertising
IN THE NATION"
_And two-thirds of it is attacking Joe..._** We have a problem, Peter. Joe's race was already a **toss-up.** Some forecasters have even said it leans Republican. name: Sabrina Singh, Democrats.org
subject: I remember when my mom became a citizen
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www
body:
My mom taught me that as long as you are doing good you should be able to stay here, work here, and live here without fear of racism and bigotry.
---
Derek --

My mom became a U.S. citizen in 1995.

At the time, I didn't realize what a big deal it was for her to become an American and give up her Indian citizenship -- but I remember how proud she was to take her citizenship test and say the same pledge of allegiance that I recited every day at school.

There are millions of immigrants who share a similar story to my mom -- people who love this country and embrace it as their own. Yet the Trump administration is hell-bent on pushing the false message that immigrants are a threat to our country and should be kept out.

That is one of the many challenges we face this year. It's going to take a ton of work to cut through the noise and talk directly with voters about who this party stands for -- immigrants, people of color, working people... the list goes on.

To get our message out, the DNC has set an ambitious goal of reaching 50 million people by November -- that's roughly the number of Democrats we need to vote in a midterm election in order to take back Congress. Reaching that many voters is expensive, and it will take serious investment. One of the best ways to do support that work is by making a monthly sustaining donation to mobilize voters this year.

**Make a $3 monthly sustaining contribution to support the DNC's efforts to reach 50 million people this year and help get them to the polls.**

[DONATE $3 MONTHLY](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522509/4067b0a/51d3d73d1/7e4b5eab/305997587/VEsHBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/march-sustainer?recurring=true&amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=10+-+DONATE+3+MONTHLY&utm_campaign=em_20180321_ss_nd&source=em_20180321_ss_nd&refcode=em_20180321_ss_nd")
Over the past year and a half, the Trump administration and Republicans have tried to pass multiple Muslim bans, tear immigrant families apart, and destroy DACA. They've emboldened white supremacists and have generally acted as if immigrants and people of color are somehow worthy of less dignity than everyone else.

This election is our chance to end this madness -- but we can't do that unless we hit our goal of reaching 50 million voters by November. It's a bold plan to move our country forward -- and frankly we won't get there without your support.

[**If you can, start a monthly sustaining contribution of $3 today and together we will mobilize a record number of voters between now and November.**](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522509/4067b0a/51d3d73d1/7e4b5ea8/305997587/VEsHBA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/march-sustainer?recurring=true&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=11+-+If+you+can+start+a+monthly+sustaining+co&utm_campaign=em_20180321_ss_nd&source=em_20180321_ss_nd&refcode=em_20180321_ss_nd")

Thanks for being a part of this,

Sabrina

Sabrina Singh
Deputy Communications Director
Democratic National Committee

P.S. -- Reaching 50 million voters is going to take people like you and me getting everyone we know to the ballot box. Tell your friends and family to commit to vote at [**IWillVote.com**](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522509/4067b0a/51d3d73d1/7e4b5ea9/305997587/VEsHBw/ "https://iwillvote.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=12+-+IWillVotecom&utm_campaign=em_20180321_ss_nd&source=em_20180321_ss_nd&refcode=em_20180321_ss_nd") today!

Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible. Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www.Democrats.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522509/4067b0a/51d3d73d1/7e4b5eb6/305997587/VEsHBg/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=13+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20180321_ss_nd&source=em_20180321_ss_nd&refcode=em_20180321_ss_nd"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522509/4067b0a/51d3d73d1/7e4b5eb7/305997587/VEsHAQ/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=14+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180321_ss_nd&source=em_20180321_ss_nd&refcode=em_20180321_ss_nd"). Click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522509/4067b0a/51d3d73d1/7e4b5eb4/305997587/VEsHAA/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=15+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180321_ss_nd&source=em_20180321_ss_nd&refcode=em_20180321_ss_nd") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522509/4067b0a/51d3d73d1/7e4b5eb5/305997587/VEsHAw/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=16+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180321_ss_nd&source=em_20180321_ss_nd&refcode=em_20180321_ss_nd"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: Bob Casey
subject: update --
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bob Casey for Senate
body:
Important update for you:
I just got off the phone with my campaign manager, and she told me that we are still $4,800 short of where we need to be to meet our quarterly fundraising goals. Our first quarter performance is critical for setting budgets, and right now, my staff is debating between cutting our advertising plan, hiring fewer staff or making smaller investments in our field operation.
That worries me, to tell you the truth, because ever since I voted against the GOP's tax scheme, they've really stepped up their attacks against me. I sided with working families, not corporations or billionaires, and national Republicans aren't happy about that. President Trump has promised to work "very, very hard" for my opponent, and last week the Koch brothers announced that they're going to run ads against me.
You've probably already heard that Democrats can take back Congress in 2018 and put a stop to the president's dangerous anti-middle-class agenda. But that will not happen if Republicans take over this Senate seat in Pennsylvania, so we need to close our $4,800 budget gap and finish the quarter strong.
Supporters like you are already making a huge difference in this race. It would mean a lot if you could take it a step further and become a campaign donor by chipping in $5 or more by midnight to help keep this race competitive.
Here's the link: **[https://secure.bobcasey.com/deadline](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e6515/51dbcc2a/e37e6a5/3a3987a/2104786137/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiYm9iLWNhc2V5IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE4MDMyMV9mcl9uZF9yZW0iLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiNSwyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=20180321_fr_nd_rem&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**

name: Frances Wolf
subject: Announcing Women for Wolf:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tom Wolf for Governor
body:
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- [](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecfad/73a2bc12/3f86bca4/1fcce8c3/3864390578/VEsH/ "http://wolfforpa.com") | Hello --

Tom knows that we're stronger when everyone is treated fairly. That's why he's been a champion for policies that ensure women aren't discriminated against when it comes to equal pay or access to birth control and comprehensive healthcare.

Republicans across the country want to turn back the clock on the progress we've made in advancing women's rights, so it's more important than ever that we stand with Tom.

**[That's why we're launching Women for Wolf -- a coalition of women supporting Tom and his pro-equality, pro-women actions -- and we're hoping you'll show your support by liking Women for Wolf on Facebook.](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecfad/73a2bc12/3f86bca4/1fcce8c2/3864390578/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/WomenForWolf")**

Tom stood up to extremists who wanted to restrict women's healthcare, and he's investing resources to combat sexual harassment and sexual violence on Pennsylvania's college campuses. He'll keep fighting for women, but he needs our support to help him continue the fight as Pennsylvania's governor.

Over the weeks and months to come, Women for Wolf will play a critical role in this campaign by organizing more events and opportunities to get involved.

So don't miss out -- **[like the Women for Wolf Facebook page now.](https://secure.wolfforpa.com/page/m/342ecfad/73a2bc12/3f86bca4/1fcce8c2/3864390578/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/WomenForWolf")**

Thanks for your support,

Frances

name: Jay Inslee
subject: A victory for reproductive health in Washington!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Sign on to celebrate Washington state's support for reproductive freedom -- and keep the momentum going. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79c95/2f5ea88d/7337128a/2cbc8d4e/2381466566/VEsH/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/reproductive-parity?utm_source=ema_2018-03-21_reproductive-parity-petition&source=ema_2018-03-21_reproductive-parity-petition&utm_campaign=reproductive-parity-petition&utm_medium=ema") Friend, Great news, Washington. After years of Republican obstruction, we finally passed the _Reproductive Parity Act_. Just today, I signed the bill into law. This is a HUGE step forward. Health insurers in Washington offering maternity care will now be required to provide a full range of reproductive health care options, including abortion coverage. **Passing the _Reproductive Parity Act_ is long overdue.** It's about time the legislature and Washington laws recognized the right of every woman to make their own personal health choices, without interference or lack of health coverage. Finally, our government is taking steps to greater ensure towards choice -- and away from unacceptable legislation over women's bodies. But we can always do more. Let's capitalize on this momentum and show the nation that it's time for similar laws in every state. [**Sign on now to celebrate the passage of the _Reproductive Parity Act_ \-- and to push for reproductive justice for every woman in America. Add your name here.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79c95/2f5ea88d/7337128a/2cbc8d4e/2381466566/VEsE/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/reproductive-parity?utm_source=ema_2018-03-21_reproductive-parity-petition&source=ema_2018-03-21_reproductive-parity-petition&utm_campaign=reproductive-parity-petition&utm_medium=ema") As governor, passing the _Reproductive Parity Act_ and protecting women's reproductive rights has been a priority of mine. So I'm proud of this victory. With women's health under attack from the Trump administration and Republicans in Congress, it's up to states to make sure health insurers doing business in Washington are mandating reproductive freedom in their coverage. [**Add your name to demand that other states take a look at the _Reproductive Parity Act_ that passed in the Washington State Legislature. We need to act now to build reproductive parity into a national movement.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79c95/2f5ea88d/7337128a/2cbc8d4e/2381466566/VEsF/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/reproductive-parity?utm_source=ema_2018-03-21_reproductive-parity-petition&source=ema_2018-03-21_reproductive-parity-petition&utm_campaign=reproductive-parity-petition&utm_medium=ema") It's time to do the right thing. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SIGN ON »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd79c95/2f5ea88d/7337128a/2cbc8d4e/2381466566/VEsC/ "https://action.jayinslee.com/page/s/reproductive-parity?utm_source=ema_2018-03-21_reproductive-parity-petition&source=ema_2018-03-21_reproductive-parity-petition&utm_campaign=reproductive-parity-petition&utm_medium=ema")

name: Senator Maggie Hassan
subject: "crisis pregnancy centers" and what you need to know
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello --
When a pregnant woman seeks medical advice, the last thing she needs is false or inaccurate information.
Across the country, women are walking into fake women's health centers, misleadingly called "crisis pregnancy centers," believing that they are receiving medically sound, neutral advice about pregnancy.
**But these centers really exist to serve one purpose: preventing a woman from accessing abortion.**
I'm proud to fight against the GOP's attempts to undermine a woman's right to access safe, informative medical care, and I need to know that you're with me. [**Will you please click here to add your name to join me in support of access to reproductive health care?**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdf3b/2a380d31/5839551/f1e052/3287566717/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20180321_CrisisPregnancy_act?source=e20180321_crisis_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
National Institute of Family and Life Advocates (NIFLA) v. Becerra, a case that will impact a woman's right to accurate information about her health care options, came before the Supreme Court yesterday, and it revolves around a California state law known as the FACT (Freedom, Accountability, Comprehensive Care and Transparency) Act.
The FACT Act mandates that organizations that give medical advice to pregnant women are honest and straightforward about the care that women can receive. That's why I joined a number of my colleagues in filing a legal brief to the Supreme Court highlighting the importance of upholding this law.
[**Please click here to sign my petition to become a citizen cosponsor of this brief and call on the Supreme Court to protect a woman 's right to accurate information about her health care options and the care she'll receive.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdf3b/2a380d31/5839551/f1e052/3287566717/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20180321_CrisisPregnancy_act?source=e20180321_crisis_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
Women who are in a vulnerable position and seeking health care deserve facts, not deception. And like any person seeking health care, they should be able to trust the information they are getting. It is essential that the Supreme Court affirms that right.
With every good wish,
Senator Maggie Hassan

The political revolution was necessary before this presidency
#
Friends,
Presidents come and go, but revolutionary movements outlast them all. The people who have shaped our laws haven't always come in the form of elected officials. The abolition, women's suffrage, and the civil rights movements were made up of dynamic activists, professors, workers, and anyone who sacrificed for the best interest of humanity.
**Behind each moment is a movement --help us sustain this one for generations yet unborn.**
[**Chip in $5 each month to become a sustaining member of Our Revolution.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ac74/bed43eb/5c422b77/75fe406b/1859552698/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-2018?refcode=em180321-full&recurring=true")
Senator Bernie Sanders told us that our campaign was never about one person or one election. It was about blazing a new trail for the tens of millions of disaffected Americans who were sick and tired of a broken political process and a rigged economy held in place by a corrupt campaign finance system.
We are the source of power behind our campaigns that spread our message to people everywhere--that is what makes our work so vital. The more we can raise to support volunteer efforts across the country, the more progressives we will elect to office. **And while the political revolution was never about one election, the truth is that this year the stakes have never been higher.**
We are at a turning point in American history. Some wish to turn their backs on the most vulnerable in our society and continue the path of division and greed that put us where we are today, but the majority of Americans want something different. With a bold progressive agenda, we can appeal to the hope and humanity that is so important to the fabric of our nation.

Your time, talent, and treasure have created an opportunity for people all across this country to take part in the political revolution. Let's seize our opportunity today.
In solidarity,
Nina Turner

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: we must persist
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __
Like many of you, my heart sank yesterday when I first learned that Great Mills High School in St. Mary's County was under lockdown and a school shooting had occurred right here in Maryland. It was another awful reminder that these shootings can happen anywhere, anytime. And it is a national disgrace that both mass shootings and the daily toll of gun violence have become so common that while we are upset by each new tragedy, we are no longer surprised.

Since the unspeakable massacre at Sandy Hook Elementary in December 2012, there have been over 300 shooting incidents in schools across our country, according to Everytown for Gun Safety. And yet parents send their kids off to school every day knowing that we have not taken all measures possible to prevent individuals who should not have access to guns from arming themselves and walking into our children's classrooms.

We cannot accept this. That's why I cancelled my meetings yesterday to drive to St. Mary's County and stand in solidarity with the students, teachers, families and first responders in Great Mills. And that's why I joined students from high schools across Maryland last week for their walkout in solidarity with students from Parkland, Florida. While words can express our feelings, only meaningful legislative action will reduce gun violence and save lives -- both from mass shootings and from the daily shootings taking place in neighborhoods across our country.

My Republican colleagues remain unwilling to consider any measures that are not rubber-stamped by the NRA, but we cannot afford to give up the fight for common sense gun laws. We must persist. Our youth are now taking the lead -- shaming those who hide behind the gun lobby and building a movement to demand action from elected officials.

Make no mistake -- this Saturday's March for Our Lives is an important moment in this fight. I will be there with students, families and activists from across our state, and I hope you will join us along with so many others to stand up for what's right.
